content:
There lives a man atop the Knoll,
Whose greasy fingers seek control;
As days go past, the man yearns for more,
Leaving his home to wander and spread his spores.
In villages he finds the ones in need,
And offering kind help, he plants the seed.
Then they find in their homes a creeping hold
A plant, a vine, a spore becoming mold.

The man returns to his forested abode,
Seeking some comfort, he finds his sickly toad.
The toad cries out, “Now let me go, I plea;
No longer do I want this grassbed home for me!”
The man grows angry and rumples at its nose;
Thus, the toad falls silent, working on its prose.

The Knoll seeks more its grasses growing hungry;
The man feeds it each day with gathered twigs dumbly.
It rumbles loud as colder nights draw near;
So he lies down with it and calls it dear.
The toad then creaks a ribbit filled with fear,
“Please, I implore you now; O lend your ear.
The Knoll is feeding on your lonely mind;
Go to the village, step outside.
In your absence I shall cleanse your home;
The Knoll will shrink, becoming a clean biome.”

The man then laughed at toad’s simple plea;
He knew the Knoll was the worst of any flea.
Yet left the home in hopes the toad was right
That such a creature small could mount a fight.
The days passed on; the toad stayed locked within,
Its throat clogged thick by workings of the Djinn.
Yet it prevailed though nearing its last breath
The Knoll now clean and void of the lethe.

As toad drifted to sleep, the man returned,
Holding a pot with brand new fronds newly un-fern’d.
Though frog had tried, the man contained the stain
A piece of Knoll embedded deep in his brain.
Thus fern took hold and gave the Knoll a path back;
The toad cried out, its lungs burst with spore-filled sacs.

Squire and Knight rode miles together. The Knight, worn thin, still carried steady flame. The squire watched him, light as a feather. “Where do we ride today?”, the squire asked. “My final place. The ground that bears my name.” The squire shrieked in fear, his master now departing. “Who will I learn from whence you are gone? Still full of wisdom left for imparting. Let me die first, for the world will not miss me.” “It will, for it is your time to be called-on” “Then, it is not your teachings but your amity I require. Watch me live as you taught, So I don't suffer grave calamity.” A crow landed on the middle road. “I want nothing more. For this I am wrought.” “Who will find me when I am lost and scared? Or pick me up and guide my way back home? There is more I need, I am lost, Impaired!” The Knight looked stoically straight ahead. “Fear not, for your heart bears my final tome.”

Pale pearl paint bled-through with ranges
of mountains and tributaries 
and beyond it, that Canadian
or Appalachian, Nordic, and Siberian.
A heavy axe leaned by a heavy door
meets the soft callus of a sleepy morning-hand
wood burns then, in a stove of stone,
as the aurora shy, starts to sing
and the snow warm starts to fall.

You sit there
or stand
Worrying about worrying
Anxious
about what you’re so sure
will come to be

And maybe
I foresee
the same

We walk here
or run
Careful about caring
Waiting
for these things to leave
for friendship

And maybe
I want
the same

But I know
this cannot stay;
that when I wake
you’re on me.

But you know
this cannot stay;
that when you wake
I’m in you.

And we lay
But feet apart
Reaching and hoping
for the other to give

Maybe it’s too real.
for you –  
And it’s probably too real.
for me –

And then, we lay
together, then apart
pulling and pushing
testing the tie

Maybe it’s too real –
No, that’s what it is;
we’ve pulled enough
and it hasn’t snapped

And we both know
this cannot stay
that when we wake
you’re on me

And I’m in you.

We walk around, here
night after night
day after day
Let’s quit the hoping
and stop the waiting

Let’s wake
like we’ve been wanting
and if we burn

We burn.

And later, they wake
         	From their Series of Dreams
 As breaths taken miles away
         	Feel like kisses on their skin.

        	They cannot see each other
         	Even though they look.
                     	They cannot hear each other
                     	Even though they listen.

And always, outside
         	Puddles sit on the sidewalk
 As yellow surrounds green
         	While the sun hides away.

        	They cannot hold each other
         	Yet, they are not cold.
                     	They cannot grasp each other
                     	Yet they know they’re there.

On and on, they go
         	For the water dances with the wind
 The green grows at the center
         	And the sun can still be found.

                                	They need not hold each other
                                 	Soon, they will be warm,
                                             	And as they long, as they reach
                                             	They know, it’s all worthwhile.

                                                                Forever they’ll live
                                                         	And ever they’ll wait.

                                                                    	And as they lay to rest,
                                                                    	They are longing to live,
                                                                                	
                                                                                	Their Series of Dreams

Way in the woods
 Surrounded by swamps
 Is a bungalow
 With a roof made of dirt
 Walls made of sand
 And a floor of mud
 It has no door
 no windows
 and inside, the same:
 no keys anywhere
 for nowhere, in there
 are there any locks.

In the bungalow
 lives a lonely man
 And everyday
 And every night
 Mothers, from the nearest town
 Fathers and sons, on the road
 Faceless passerby
 And friendly bygones
 Come by
 Some for minutes
 Some for hours
 On their own volition.

With this man
 They open their hearts
 Spill their minds
 And empty their souls.
 And to them
 He gives ears
 He gives eyes
 He gives space
 And, he gives time
 And, he stays,
 never diverging
 until they are done.

And, when they do leave
 And there’s a moment’s rest
 The man lays in the mud
 and he cries.

Even with the oncoming sun
 and the spring it brings
 along with the snow, melting
 turned to soft slush
 making nice puddles for jumping
 and filling the rivers
 for swimming.

Even then:
 with the new enlightenment
 and its sound wisdoms
 along with the new rhythm
 blowing sax and singing soul
 slinging sweet symphonies
 and beating the bell.

How,
 amongst the wiring
 and amid the welding 
 which craft frames
 and draw schematics
 does this all seem
 like semantics?

How, then
 submerged in this salt
 floating along the flotsam
 which, rotting
 smells like a memory
 Seldom lived
 and often remembered?

Maybe, then
 this ardent zeal
 is blasphemous bloat
 tacked on and taped
 to entice the big snake
 that it’s better to sleep
 than to slither.

Or, maybe,
 There isn’t a sheen
 But a sheet of sheens
 and those already passed
 in the past, should stay
 and some select others
 are saved solely for God.


Can you hear me?
 Wherever you are?
 I tell you –
 and it’s all gone

Do you agree?
 how seamless
 and simply strange
 dreams are?

Does your reality
 destabilize and estrange
 blending into dreams
 and blending forth from them?

Is boredom so terrible?
 Relative to hunger?
 Relative to loneliness?
 You’d rather have nothing?

And then
 as the fuselage explodes
 do you imagine
 being stranded at sea?

Floating along
 with shark passerby
 and gulls overhead
 all alone


To then be collected
 and be returned
 so people celebrate
 your own name?

You parents’ embrace
 A friend’s kiss
 and a lover’s touch
 the ocean far away

Do you, then
 wish it were closer
 imagine, then
 you were sailing once again?


Later, can you imagine
 on your death bed
 flowers on the table
 your family gone

The room all white
 Reeking with nothing
 and visiting hours
 not scheduled until later

Can you think?
 what is later.
 when is it?
 and why, then, do you even care?

Can you hear me?
 Because you will
 Dying and alone, you’ll wish
 You were sailing at sea

You will, then
 wish things were closer
 and imagine, then
 you were sailing once again.

Across the stars
 never has there been
 and never will there be
 something quite like this

One moment, it’s day
 The next, it’s night
 Miss the sunset
 and miss the between.

In its reality
 It’s become unreal
 To that, at home
 and its gentle hold

So gentle
 and so warm.

Remember this
 and hunt for it
 try to hold on
 - never will it pass again

One moment, it’s day
 The next, it’s night
 Miss the sunset
 and miss the between.

In its reality
 It’s become unreal
 From there, the home
 and all you’ve ever known

So gentle
 and so warm.

From this and the next
 Forevermore, it’s gone
 Search for the spirit
 Sated, this will never be

One moment, it’s day
 The next, it’s night
 Miss the sunset
 And miss the between

In its reality
 It’s become unreal
 To that, the heart
 So carefully cared for

So gentle
 and so warm.

Across the stars
 never has there been
 and never will there be
 anything quite like this.

One moment, it’s day
 The next, it’s night
 Missing the sunset
 We’ve missed the between.

In its reality
 It’s become unreal
 To that, the mind
 and all we now know.

So gentle
 and so warm.

So gentle
 that we wish we were
 So warm
 all that’s gone wrong.

So gentle, and so warm,
 that we wish we were
 So gentle, and so warm,
 All we know we’re not.

How long will I rot
 until I leap from this bog
 - until I realize
 I can indeed smell
 the stinking smog
 and I can indeed read
 the bloody writing
 sprayed on every building
 etched into every brick
 and shining in the oil
 flooding every parking lot
 and built from the bodies
 filling every city ditch;
 cut into our faces
 and scarred into our thighs
 burned into our bottles
 and scabbed into our scalps
 poisoning our minds
 and infesting our gods
 bastardizing our sons
 and damming our daughters
 and, everywhere else
 written on every wallowing wall.
 How long, will I rot?
 until I realize
 I can, with my own eyes
 unfettered, indeed
 and unfiltered
 unbiased
 and with unaimed prejudice
see all this for what it is.

How long will I rot
 until, from what I know
 I finally act
 How long, will we rot?
 until we finally decide -
 from all we feel from the forests
 and from what we’ve derived
 for so long
 from the deserts;
 from all the skies have said -
 not what’s been contrived
 but what they’ve screamed
 for centuries;
 from all the blood we’ve spilt
 and from the wars we’ve waged
 for no other reason
 than that’s all we know;
 from all the science has shown -
 not that which we’ve decided
 but that, to it
 we’ve assigned truth;
 and from our faiths
 all and one; one and all
 and from our passions
 all and one; one and all
 do we finally decide
 we must make something else.

If it must be
 then let it be a war
 if it must
 then why wait for the war?

A war
 with the forest’s wishes
 and the sky’s blessings.
 A war
 where the blood shed
 is not red
 but black;
 where the science
 ceases to be
 A war
 where our faiths
 and where our passions
 bring down not buildings
 but blow up how little
 we’ve managed to imagine
 A war, not final
 but to begin anew.

How long, must we rot
 until we realize
 we must do it ourselves?

We were never where we were supposed to be
 We never knew
 and never cared
 now, I know
 and now, I care

 I wish
 we could go together.

They’re those left behind;
 those expected yet neglected.
 Forced to douse their lamps
 and made to sail us through the seas.

Unto them, were instilled storms:
 beaten out of them, were their bones
 sucked from them, were their souls
 Lulled away, was their love.

We’re underdecks
 we’re off hidden in the dark.
 Against their arms, are the oars
 as they bear the brunt of the winds.

The acidic rain’s sulfuric;
 it’s raking and tearing at their flesh.
 The rotting bread’s wet;
 scattered, the rats hoard the crumbs.

The frozen winds howl;
 it’s eroding at their skulls.
 The black waves bring sickness;
 a few more have drowned away.

Muffled their voices are
 shouting and calling through the planks –
 through their welded muzzles
 and sewn-shut hoods.

Stumbling over every swell
 Is our odyssey doomed.
 Down here, a few drops leak through
 So few, are those piercing the net.

The storms are perfect
 in our science and design:
 they’re beyond atomic;
 their fallout will be everlasting.

They’ll eventually leave
 having succumbed to the storm:
 they’ll be sentenced to the sea
 face-up, or face-down.

We’re underdecks
 we’re off hidden in the dark.
 And we’ve our own lamps
 and we’ll have grown enough food.

 There are so few galleons, now
 and the little leaks are so many
 is nobody left
 to sail our sinking ship?

To our odyssey doomed
 Has our storm
 Really killed
 All who could’ve landed her?

 Where have they gone?
 Those that would have?
 With solely a spoon
 Those that would have?

My addictive friend
 shivers in the summer.
 It whimpers as you sing
 and itches as ears ring.

I’m a turnstile
 slapping right to left
 and lurching back again
 between this vice
 and that well-worn tune
 with its weathered notes
 and whose melody
 was never that good
 anyway.

My addiction
 shivers in the summer.
 Please, one more time sing
 so I may feel that ring.

I’m a workhorse
 trudging from here to there
 and lurching back again
 between this task
 and my newest obligation
 with its tired rhythms
 and whose steps
 remind me awfully
 of my old music.

I, alone
 shiver in the summer.
 I’ll bury it under anything
 so my ears don’t ring.

Please, one more time, sing
 Sing for me
 So I may hear that ring

Friend
 who’s tired and sad
 Trying so hard to seem hard
 There’s respite in us
 and you may not show it
 but I know you know
 and this, you know too.

My friend,
 you spend the night taking.
 You’re so scared of sharing
 from you, so much was stolen
 and when you reached out last
 open-handed
 you were beaten down again.

Friend
 you hide yourself
 like you’ve always done
 and these people you take from
 and those taking from you
 likely, they’ll never know
 you’ve even a mask.

My friend,
 you’re too stubborn to stop
 and too stuck-in to ask
 May you find someone
 who, against your shell
 will bring a shovel
 and you may share with them
 that which you always were.

My beautiful baby
 Hands on the pavement
 and feet in the grass
 Eyes tied shut
 And hair let down
 its bright waves gold
 falling over your shoulders
 and settling
 just past your breast
 When you turn away
 And, head high
 Eyes open, then
 And your fingers feeling
 Keep going
 and don’t ever stop.
 When, after the storm,
 the grass tickles your toes
 And, when,
 after the drought
 it scratches your skin
 let it burn
 and let it melt
 Let it all come apart
 until your ashes
 swept up
 by that sudden wind
 blow away
 until all that’s left
 is scattered all over
 by that little lightly hue
 and still;
 let it burn
 and let it melt
 But please
 My beautiful baby
 don’t ever
 not even for a thought
 begin back down that way
 that took everything
 and abandon this:
 these gifts
 of something.

Across the tablecloth
 I foresee what will be said;
 I know what’s been shared
 and i even
 relate
 to you

And I say nothing
 No words your way
 just expressions
 diluted and reserved
 muted
 muzzled

I used to wait for a question
 To which, I’d answer
 But now, that’s gone
 I hold back
 deeming it simple
 too easy

At this table
 I don’t make it ours
 I leave it yours
 only waiting
 to leave

I lay
 and wish I spoke
 I could and I can
 but, I’m back there
 and i long to leave again.

I tell you this
 in doing so
 I adhere to my heart.
 All those things I’ve offered
 the suggestions and lessons
 that you’ve adopted
 and have, I think,
 healed with –
 I validate.
 I live truthfully
 through my dogma
 I’ve so zealously
 enticed you to.

I tell you this
 and I’m not a hypocrite.
 I do this thing
 and
 it’ll be me
 following my heart
 that droning machine
 clicking on every hour
 rimming through the night
 I’d be unplugging it
 soothing that itch
 you so easily
 just like every time
 before
 enflamed.

I tell you this
 and everything’s back on
 we’re back on the track
 running our miles;
 walking with our moon
 and fishing our creeks.
 Once more, that fox,
 will sit before us
 and cock its head
 as those fireflies
 mate in the night.

I
 don’t
 tell you this
 and you keep your picture:
 you keep your pretty smile
 and all I’ve given you
 you
 get to keep;
 I don’t tell you
 and I’m left with that
 machine
 which I’ll leave
 when I finally sail away.

He’s left his swamp
 and his bank in the road
 behind him, are his passerby
 before him, now
 are the sleepless.

He waits
 and they reach out
 and he gives a finger
 and then a hand
 and then his head.

And he returns
 to his tower
 bricks and shingles
 leaving open his blinds
 and his door.

He's gone on too long
 Too proficient are his lies
 Too convincing
 is his face
 No one ever says hello.
pdfs/Ae'elles.pdf

You look me in that way
 and whisper to your friends
 behind closed hands
 just enough
 of your mouth
 so I may know.

We all want someone
 not always the same
 sometimes, it’s so
 I know enough
 and feel enough
 to know you feel for me.

But, does it flow?
 how may I know –
 with what’s barely come
 and besides, all this
 is not new;
 I’m recently versed.

I’m versed in that.
 not this.
 you are new; and i
 am not what I was
 I’m versed in those things
 You, are new.

Old roads have alleys
 with basements and attics
 glowing red
 and whispering spells
 whom am I
 to say I’ve seen your sound?

I think then, of you
 and find little:
 no room to deliberate
 no problem to solve
 and no mysteries to ponder
 Perhaps, I’m blind.

Moreso, I find
 I’ve walked well this road
 and, this time,
 You’ve the leash
 gently, leading me back
 as I hold it in one
 while twitching the other.

Something is near
 keeping me from my feet;
 from those cobbled streets,
 my darkened woods
 and bright groves.

That thing on the stairs
 crouching child, nearly young
 skin charred black
 head between its knees
 eyes down
 clothes so ragged
 they look like skin
 for so small a slip
 I see it sit
 there, on those steps,
 so help me
 I see you again, tomorrow
 I’ll know nothing more.

That thing on the stairs
 I see you again
 is it you?
 or is it me?
 maybe there’s no difference
 none anymore
 anyway.

It's near
 just away;
 in this way
 so seldom quick enough
 to see you there, sitting
 on your stairs
 you look at me
 with your head down
 funny, though
 I don’t even
 want you to leave
 don’t think I really care.

You come into my room
 you’ve done it before
 before the ocean
 now, here too
 I cannot escape
 the first time, I didn’t mind
 I welcomed your company
 you’re back again;
 rather, I’ve found you
 again
 well
 hello.

You’re a funny little thing
 I’m a funny little thing
 so help me if I see you
 tomorrow.

There’s this thing following me
 just out of sight
 I feel it, now,
 through closed blinds
 and my propped door
 which closes with a glance
 you pretend to hide
 you do this on purpose
 I do this on purpose, too
 it’s our game.

Where there was pity
 confused to be sympathy
 is now contempt
 manifested and realized
 from this burning core;
 that pool you swim from
 is shallow
 this gaze, drawn long
 is sharp
 accommodations are given
 like apologies
 between an older brother
 and a sobbing girl,
 smiles are produced
 not within
 but for without,
 you may hold my hand
 but I
 not yours.

There is much pain here
 You are my anti-Christ
 So far away
 I will never have you
 Only in death, may I come close
 besides,
 in death
 you’d kill me anyway.

You gloat to me
 Wide-eyed, open-mouthed
 waving arms and walking legs
 about how much
 you love to fuck.
 With who?
 Not with me.
 On this mission
 I’m missing you
 Oh, how I know
 When I see you next
 I’ll want to fold
 Either into you
 or by myself –
 By myself
 because you’re too busy
 doing what you love.

The last breath
 Stopping the snap
 it, only seconds,
 before it severs
 Draw this breath
 you command
 or else, disappointment;
 Draw this breath
 you say
 or else, manipulation;
 Force this breath
 you command
 or else, more of the same:
 We draw this breath
 and we are done –
 there’ll be nothing more
 to look forward to
 Heh
 We drew this breath
 now look!
 look where we are.
 You’re scarcely yourself;
 Without seeing you
 these past three years
 and I still, can tell
 that which you always refused.
 And me, I can’t stop
 all I am is myself
 and I find something new
 and can’t help being reminded
 of you;
 I can’t help remembering
 how much I had
 how much lingers
 and how little
 you still are;
 how little you were
 anyway.

I lay in my coma
 Within this temporary cell
 My externals burn
 heat’s filled my face
 itchiness taps my lungs
 rot’s consuming my signatures
 torn down, my defense is

 against myself,
 anyway, no longer
 self-respect, is there?
 If it’s there
 it's not my own.

Others sit, and I stand
 not awake, yet
 and I care not to
 Every feeling’s falsified
 every expression a joke
 every laugh’s forced
 and the smiles expected

Unto you
 when you finally dock
 here, eyes closed
 my heat will pass
 And I will open.

I’ve made it past the shallow
 These waves, no smaller
 Aren’t any bigger
 And there, before me:
 Those great big seas
 With all their oceans
 All filled to the top

I dip under
 the hair on my head, wet
 And I see reefs
 great beds of coral
 filled with fishes and eels
 little shells and brittle stars
 and colors painted pretty

A little further, now
 I paddle and swim
 There, before me:
 A great school of manta rays
 The giant jellyfish
 One whole leviathan
 It, to those deep depths

I’m yanked back.
 You, holding the other end
 This cable, wrapped around
 and caught my stomach.
 You are my last tether
 and my final reminder
 of my life on the land.

Laying under our passion tree
 Passing the purple fruits
 We, stumbling over,
 Have happened upon.

It streams from your lips
 Smelling like sweet-sense
 and tasting like wine
 My eyes fall sleepy

All these tall flowers
 And there, a horse by the creek
 you lean and kiss
 And me, the same.

The truth I seek:
 found in gurus and mystics,
 carved into cracks at the backs of caves
 and cast starry across a mountain’s night;
 hidden somewhere, formless,
 deep within some dark seething beast
 and still, then, only a clue
 a mere suggestion of what may be.

Those things you find
 printed pretty on colored paper
 and coded carefully
 so simply stumbled over
 and yet, heralded as prophets
 returning, finally, and fortunately,
 to luminate this otherwise
 unknown and unfathomable void.

Look down and find the feet
 and there still, on the floor
 Open your mouth
 and feel the moisture cool
 Take a little tumble
 and feel some bones break
 Open your eyes, just some,
 and see past what’s shown.

So hesitant to send
 away our seventh son
 the prodigal Adam, son of God
 the imagination’s machination.

 There goes Aryus
 Scaling the towers of stone
 and honing his grovel
 with flesh and hands.

And there, enters Armen
 And they walk and fish
 Hail traffic and take the train
 and suddenly, Soolaimon.

So suddenly, one starry night
 Comets carrying the sky
 and in its still
 there between, Soolaimon.

So scared to send
 and so forth our seventh son
 the prodigal Adam, son of God,
 for fear he will return.

Disillusioned so long
 trampled, beaten, and berated
 belittled and disrespected
 ripped, severed, and shamed
 opened, peeled, and filled
 to be thrown away again.

Over and over
 for years and years
 Spitting so I may swallow
 Circling so I may fall
 To these and them, couch and bed
 never me, and never us

A strange sunset
 that I surely have found
 Something so surreal
 To flee, is my instinct,
 to refuse, to abandon
 to jump away and drown.

But welcome… this new sun
 against the cold so set, so warm
 so good, so tender, and so sweet;
 melt this demeanor stoned
 and let the heart I once owned
 be once more mine.

Precautions, now, disregarded
 Not necessarily ever needed
 But now, suddenly, such is obvious
 They creep up and are buried
 These precautions, now, surely,
 need not be heeded.

Meddled mind but clear wants
 With no less restraint or control;
 With no less vision and honor
 rather, something new,
 A complete and utter regard
 For what may be manifested

Given I just go.

Eyes wide-tired
 Friends from home
 and home is not here
 Home is long ago
 and there is nothing here
 From all that before.

Something fixes everything
 Is there and ready
 Pluck it, simply
 Pick it and eat it
 From you, it’s already grown
 Into you, it has long been bound.

Your issue is mine
 Your concern is otherworldly
 of demons and faeries
 When all the fantasy
 and all your fruit
 are waiting to be had.

Believe our words
 His, if not mine,
 and theirs’, if not these
 A friend is like family
 One friend, a brother
 A friend, is a friend

Your consideration
 Merely one of my many
 So thoughtful, so fruitful
 How kind, and how caring.

I claim nothing
 And you plant your stake
 into my open chest
 Ignorant to your arrogance.

 You cannot fathom
 These thoughts, of mine,
 This little counter you claim
 Is barely a whisper.

You appear as a friend
 And consider yourself such
 And I, the same
 I believe you.

My judgment burns
 Opinions and beliefs, a doctrine
 Of which is my own
 And have kept it such.

And, truly, you are clueless
 I refrain whilst you proclaim
 So many times, so many years
 and you abandon me.

You dare say you’re jealous
 Try living your own life
 You dare whistle suicide
 You can try living mine.

 Maybe I should be temperate…
 God forbid I’m angry
 And God forbid, I feel good
 Just once.

I think I remember reading somewhere
 When you’re down and tired
 You return to things since shed
 So long ago
 or yesterday

Exhaustion
 Skin is hot
 Brain is running
 poking, flowing, melting
 I can’t see
 I can’t think
 I’m down
 and I’m low

Counting the minutes
 Hoping they would pass
 So I may waste the rest
 and continue to itch

Scratch and pick
 Tear and bleed;
 If only I would bleed
 Out, in little pools
 From my filthy pores
 Acid coagulated
 Stiff and full
 Stuck and filling

I’m realizing myself

And perforated the sole precious truth.
Then, amongst the red trumpets and applause
Globe in one hand, goblet in the other
All these people, and an empty seat beside me
I stared and I lost the sound.

I was without time. 
Every drink devisable, and every sip conceivable;
Every odorous rise, and every sweet-smelling fall;
Anything a man may eat
And then, anything a man may feel
And then, anything a man may be
I consumed it all.

Memory was a pained reminder
Then, I needed reminding of the memory.
After that, I wished for that small sensation
How a single tear would trickle, at some sad song.
Wood, stones, fire, and bones
Bones, fire, stones, and wood
All the same, and the same was all.

Music filling my ears
A song, then a song not at all.
In a wished-for fever, I commissioned a vessel
I stepped foot on that abandoned beach
Then, like the very day
I made for that spot between those cliffs
And there, I sat and cried. 

Music filling my ears
A song, then a song not at all.
From a memory I was feeling, I set sail
And I stumbled upon that abandoned beach
I wandered lost, until I was suddenly aware
I stared, seeing through the fog surrounding me
At some vision that was not there. 

Sound reaching my ears
A song, then not.
I woke up on that abandoned beach
I was amongst limestone homes
I was mooring a raft, stepping onto that beach
I was sitting, between the cliffs, at a spot in the land
I turn to leave. Then I am there again. 

My eyes open, and I am somewhere else
Every organism, every single microcosm
Perceived and dissected with disturbing perfection
Until the revulsions, the satisfactions, the perversions
All that too, fading into the dawnbreaker’s dusk.
There, again, a vision - a single silhouette. 
An old man, he waits for me. 

I follow him, taking to the wing
Seeing him now, his beard is full, and his hair is long
But his face bears no lines
And his wings are as swift as mine.
I open my mouth to speak
And I hear the words he says to me -
I heard the words he said to me. 

“I want to show you,” he said,
“Did you think you were the only one?”
And as we sail down to a newborn isle
Passing like wisps, but material nonetheless
There are people, and only a few look our way
Nowhere has age graced them
Despite the life departed from their eyes. 

I remember then, in an eternity’s instant
My own tears, how they flowed
When I held my lover in the highlands;
“I want to say, that I am sorry,” the man said to me,
“Although those things, you cannot feel
They cannot, and I cannot either
I could argue that that sorrow is sensible
Maybe at one time, there may have been some sentiment
All this, everything; if you have not already
You will come to know.
This, you well understand;
I could admit, if it were as such
That this was my desperation.”

My mouth makes no words
My companion is beside me, and I do not see him
Everywhere and nowhere; everything and nothing.
I remember all that ever happened.
I know all that’s happening.
I recall all that I felt, and the flame’s faded.
I am out of time.

Okay, here it goes
I am adrift down the sea
Ship’s gone, I am my vessel
Alone, I’ve gone numb
Gonna throw up my lifeline. 

Remember, everything in order
I saw her, just ahead;
I called to her, she turned her head
Side by side, we walked
Happy as friends would be.

Watching movies my comrades could not believe
Settling into circumstances
That my confidante, for, would have dreamed
Smiling, he patted my back, leading me home
All while I could not look him in the eye;
As directed, as prescribed, all those details
Perfectly organized, as per a miracle
And yet, all I could do was eat -
Finally together, all I could do was sleep.
Over you, I studied your eyelids
How your lips would be just apart
The way your breath met the air
And listened, and listened, for you to whisper;
Then, when you opened your eyes,
You would never see
But I rushed to close mine. 

Infested, rot filling my gut
Following mine, finding freedom, I pulled the plug
And I think of your pain, now
And I wonder, instead, if I had laid my head
But I remember now, the world opened
I think of this only as I try to close it;
I feel this only when I am falling. 

I would have wanted to have stayed your friend
To not be afraid to call your name, and to walk as we did.
I told you once, I see myself out if I serve no purpose
Exile myself, if there’s no explicit justification
But friendship is explicit enough. 

There was one before
She is the one that lurks in the swamp
Emerging, rising, at the pit of the fall
All to sink away, into the dark;
I remember everything she ever did. 

And as I reached and clawed, I blamed her hatefully
I hung myself, hopelessly holding her memory
Attributing the death of you and I to that past. 
But for all that torture, that second-hand, the doubt and fear
Every sorrow - she was the icon with which I bled myself. 

Remember now, everything blown apart
Picking up my shaking pieces, I picked you out
Measuring, postulating, theorizing - I charted a course;
In my head exactly, I wrote that plot, I drew up that storyboard;
I envisioned a great insurrection - and then, a holy vindication. 

As written before, you were no saint, and I am no satan
I was a Lucifer - proud beyond prideful, and ambitious beyond arrogance.
I had the means too, but thank God I failed
With you, Beatrice, I would have fallen far beyond Hell.
I am wiser, now, but, back then, I was by no means a fool.

Unto you, fiery retribution will fall
But not by my hand, for I refuse to be that man
However it may be, I hope you find it on your own.
Innocent though, just and pure, was that I destroyed;
May I be strong enough, so I may, one day, help in some way
And, when I leave, may you never know. 

May I stand trial.
May I be stood, nailed, and may I burn for my crimes,
While I live witness for each of my sins.
Your love, and your care - you are in my heart
Your words - I will keep them in my head.
All that time, that nearly tenured, with each of you, 
What exactly have I lost?
With all of you, all you people, and every slept-walked hollow
Where did I stumble, how did I feel, and what have I found?
Farewell my sums and pieces - I’ll not be around
This tired melodrama, along with the last, are coming to a close
I remember what I lost, and I know what I have found.
I will arrange every precious piece, every dear desire
Hold them with honor, and support them with steel
If you want me, then follow my lifeline
That passed is yours. 
These words, these acts, they are mine.

I am the maestro, the master, and the miser
In the back, the jealous Kaiser
And in the light, the lovely entertainer. 

This place piled-over with papers.
Is the intelligent mind one which mines
All that buried beneath beyond the core?
Or is the intelligent man the one who maps
Seeking beyond the sky, sailing beyond the stars - 
He who understands he’d rather work with his hands.

Many sleepless nights spent planning
And this tired day lived dreaming. 

Remembering that long time running
Walking on this cold Texas morning. 

A weary woman lies weeping
And a broken man’s bid to bear;
He is the lantern bringer
Appealing alone by the lectern;
She, alone, guards that answer
Appearing regal beside the cistern. 

With tomorrow’s dawn, we will be riding
All along and through Los Palos - 
We will go wandering.

Early that forgotten morning
Up, at the top of our tower
You’re golden, asleep on my chest
My arm around your shoulder.

I hope I may love someone
Half as much as I love you.
I watched that sleepy rising sun
And I gently hold you.



Thirty-one days from Raddigan’s
Two years and two months from Maddigan’s
And one day from my birth date.
Twenty minutes stared by
With more hour left to drain. 

“Here’s why”
“Yeah, that’s why”
“Well, it’s one reason.”
I used to be twenty
And I’m bound to be twenty-two.

Your heart’s a pretty diamond
And I don’t know what I said on our walk
But I remember liking what you told me
And I know we wanted to walk a little further;
I will be alive a little while longer
Through your heart, I don’t want ink to bleed
And turning, I don’t want it to tip and fall;
You asked me my preference, aisle or center
Saying it’s a shame the front’s so full.

Well, I wouldn’t mind being in the back
Left, right middle, it’s really up to you
Anywhere in there - as long as it’s next to you.
I don’t want to break your diamond heart
But all we’re doing is walking to the theater
And all I promised was to buy you a drink after.

The House upon the Sun always sets:
A synagogue grown from the sand
From a forest sprout ripped from the land
Where bound are the bones of our bets. 

My body is the ammunition with which I destroy my soul
And I am the demon of hatred. 
I will temper myself into a weapon
So to earn next week’s end.

Dreary down a darkened hall
The tunnel gives to a sheet of snow
A low whistle, a covered face
Grayed black hair, still in the wind. 

I am a low warm red
The growing hue on the morning dew,
The dark black of that swirling center,
The bright blue of the dying day.

Running on the edge of this blade
Perusing for a means to persuade. 
Hammering a nail into a hole
Fabricating reasons to save my soul.

The Big Wednesday
Tending behind a bar.
Then, wander down the ruins
Life on our lips and in our throats
Blood glowing through our skin. 

Oh, to spend a year
Or maybe two, or maybe three
Hanging out on the beach,
Surfing on sunset waves,
And waking up beside the fire.

Brief colliding with grief
I will take you as you are;
Hear me now for what I am. 
A comet shot blisteringly blue
Swayed by dark sinks in space
Blasting out beyond this frozen belt. 
I am here, and you are there:
All that could be built of infrastructure
Could not contain the core of the world.

I am a vibrating wrecking ball. 
I can do anything
And I feel like less than nothing
“Share everyone your favorite number”
I could not manage even to whisper. 

I am the ripping singularity
A lead fridge barreling toward the Earth
A great bird walking alone at night. 

I am the morning star
Twinkling pretty above the sea; 
Last night, I drove to the hospital. 
You come closer, I hide my face
Of myself, I will leave no trace.

Finally setting to sail
On a vessel titled Termina
All to sit in the center of the Pacific
Which reaches ‘till it stops.

Those four years on the scaffolds
An apprentice then, as such is now
The worst of this Earth, I had met
But was I ever any different? 

Preaching and demanding this and that
While spreading and serving the same;
Playing the hero, and begging the victim
Whilst never letting anything fall free. 

Their minds, infecting with expectations
The truth and reality - all that you feign
Building prisons with plastic bricks;
Refusing to believe all that could be.

Pulling air deep into my throat
To let it bubble back up
Looking down over the ledge
I wheeze and spit and choke. 
Sitting here, I hear it crackle
That consumption, I can taste
My flat smells like cigarettes
And my glass is on the floor. 

Yes, but you had laughed at me
And now, I can pour a beer
Soon, I will smile at you
And although I am not there yet
I wished you Bon Matin
And I could feel it in my heart.
I am snapping off that dread
And chiseling that dug-in
I will with me, bring my gin
Cut this chord, and wrap that wire:
Under those great conifers
And alongside clay chasms
Upon the land of my blood
And destined for the land of our’s
I will listen to the songs of the sea. 

Awake for a fortnight
And I cannot sleep.
There I laid, the light of the clock
Like the nighttime trains along my wall.
Pictures of me, and all my friends
And my family, they’re here with me. 
You passed the cigarette - and now, I look away
I want to be the boy from neverland.

I am home early now
I only drank four drinks
And I am bound now to bed
My eyes still hurt
My head still aches
And my heart still bleeds. 
My friends are speaking
I am listening 
And I am waiting for the end
I am licking envelopes
To a man I can’t help but like
And hiding behind each letter.
My friend complimented me
He called me a poet
And against my protests
Despite my professions
He insisted it was swell
And that’s the nicest thing I’ve ever been told. 
I feel so sad
But I refuse to be weak
To be anything less than the best;
When I arrived, I was the student
Maybe once, I was helpless
But now, I am the master.

I dreamt you wanted to kiss me
I told you I didn’t
But then I lifted you to my lips
And you tasted wet and soft.
I carried you alongside a forest
Across a field filled with holes
Your legs were around my hips
And my hands held you up by yours. 

I dreamt we were wanting to escape
Every way blocked, I took a key
Coming then to another dead-end
I turned, confessed, and it I returned.
I dreamt then I was on trial
Under accusation I had stolen something
After stern scrutiny, and with rolled eyes
I was reluctantly pronounced freed.

I remember my hawkish gaze
How I had swooned your vacancy
Then into that void, I filled
Via strength and pity, that which I vied. 
I dream I am laying on your lap
And when I sleep, the morning shines with you. 
I walk down the block, seeing if you’re home;
I sit now in the street, dreaming of you.

If there was something I believed in, 
I’d probably do it. 
Waiting for a time to turn the tide
This preservation is without self
Just a determination for our tomorrow. 

I’ve been bored-broken these seven years
Taught to destroy of that for I stand
Learned to burn all that for I may yearn.
My drinking tempers the derision
If I had more money, I’d drown in the sea.

Tempered to a fine point
Sharpened as an arrow
Shining against the sun
Blaring just barely blue. 

Firing off like a fucking rocket
Pure heat - ashing down the sky;
Saying hello to all my friends
I’m coming crashing through the day. 

Those nights, I went to the bar
And I drank, burning through my bread.
Tonight, I’m going back to the bar
And I will talk, laugh, and play pool instead. 

Those nights, we withered our wicks… 
No. Our line, scared, I severed. 
But this is the New Age
The New Age of the Blazing World. 

I am a nuclear warhead
Come strolling with me, up this mountainside
I will shatter this city to glass
And raze the weeds back to the plains. 

Lead me, by my hand, wherever you wish
In your every step, I will reside. 
Tomorrow, we will wade down the river
And the warmth’ll glow along our sides. 

Lay with me in the death of day
Touch me, and kiss me upside down
We’ll take turns, filling each other up
And when we wake, hold me with the dawn.

Underneath the waning moon
He’s about ten; and her, no more than nine
Yesterday the mine - tomorrow, the tower
But tonight, they track the loon.

Soon, they are atop their tree
Hand in hand, they share a branch
Hearts light, eyes full and free
They shine with their moon upon their lake. 

Then, the boy and girl are grown
They’re married, at weddings they both remember
Vows proven in pictures, she stares at the wall
As he lifts a purple frown. 

But for an afternoon every month
As the day fades on their satin stools
Little envelopes, they gently open
And lull careful over each letter.
They read and imagine every line
And feeling warm, they rise and go to hide
Their hearts in the box they keep in a chest. 

Later, wrinkles in their hands
With warm coffee and tea, they share their bench;
Together, while kids laugh and play in the park
A boy and girl run, and they both smile.

Tomorrow, she’s on the train
And him, he’ll be setting sail to somewhere;
Tomorrow, they both know, forecast’s calling rain
And along with the day, together they wane. 

Him, he is about fifty
She’s no more than forty-nine 
Souls light, they’re hand-in-hand
Shining with their moon upon their lake.

Atomic annihilation on the horizon
And I’m in radiation withdrawal;
Head ringing, my phone’s buzzing
I figure it’s time to steal a handgun
As I wonder about your drawl. 

For the first time in my life
And finally, I feel there’s some stakes;
Smiling, I lap up my boiling blood
I consider where I should set to go
Just as always - I cut my brakes.

I am not your gypsy.
Dressed in flowers and fleurs
But bewildered by nearing the road. 

I have no money
But I have my family
All behind, and all ahead of me.

The river along the railroad
Balls of fire, far beyond the trees
And rocks resting on the riverbed.
I am a gypsy, and I will sleep
Amongst the grassy sands of the shore
And on the concrete step of the street. 

I am no one’s gypsy
Dressed in whatever I can find
Tailored to fit that that’s mine. 

I am my own gypsy
Blowing smoke out a wooden window
My face tight with fever’s sorrow.

Every gallon in the sea
Of every forest, every tree
And in the desert, my brother’s being wed. 
I am a gypsy, and I will journey
I’ll go till I’ve no more blood to bleed
I’m embarked beyond bewilderment.

Wake me up in the morning
I’ll be your baby in the evening. 
All that in the divide
In the dirt, dug deep and wide
Across it, I’ll crawl in the dark
I’ll be waiting, whittling our mark.

Banding together to beat a dog
You turned to me, asking for permission
Evidently, I know him best. 
Well, you could have asked him
Instead of judgment on a whim
Concealed with smiles until he’s gone. 
So afraid to start a fire
You complain, refusing to limp
As you scoff behind puffs of smoke.

Looking up at me like a puppy
Crawling up to me, all meek
Expecting to receive some shard of my soul. 
Well, it was cracked off a mirror
One I’d painted over in blue
And is now stained a splotch of red.
I never had to follow you
Burn my reserves, or destroy my body
None of this is that you’ve earned. 

Masquerading a mark of the wolf
This lightless shell, wrapped in wool
Reamed with the rot of a dead animal. 
Well, you mock whom with you lay
Lean upon heresy, begging for the floor
And desecrate any point I may make.
I possess a massive, useless manuscript
My holy grail - the means to my grace;
The more this hurts, the better I bleed. 

Following a fox through a field
There’s flame along the sky, warm and low
And a stream of stones up the mountainside.
Well, you’ve set your life to science
Aligned yourself halfway to summation:
You can have yours - I will make what’s mine 
and tomorrow morning, truth shall shine.
I am in the dark beside a bonfire
Red in my lap, the man with a beating heart.

Counting every minute
Chewing on single seconds
Watching the clock crawl
Within a blink, it’s nightfall. 
A vacation spent by
The volume inside rings
Flesh is weak, and muscles whine
The sickness inside spreads.
A week pissed by
Much to do, and more isn’t done
I’ll be back soon; then, moved on
What about me isn’t enough?

Beyond fucking angry. 
Beyond fucking inadequate. 
How horribly did I terrify you?
How terribly did I hurt you?
Golden, diamond-crested crosses
And one thousand souls sealed in silk;
I’ve my own brazen bull
Abandoned cold beside some ditch.
The moment the light dies
Rapped are dry, cracking breaths
As the blood left turns to bone
And every heartbeat starts to hurt.

We landed in Dallas ten hours ago
A good friend, he picked me up
We sipped coffee, watching people go
Out of red cans, we drank syrup. 

I’ve been at my desk ten minutes
Half-finished bottle at my feet
Another friend, he’s hanging ornaments
And I just told him that’s rather sweet. 

I’m replaying this song its tenth time
Wondering, in passing, of his Christmas shopping
Thinking, about how cocaine rhymes with brain
Taping together the ten times I’ve finished this evening. 

There’s a throbbing, where my leg meets my hip
And a dulled, faded burning in between.
Later, I’ll feel the seeping remnants drip
The little flame, falling familiarly into that sheen.
I walk into the wall, my life shattering on the floor
And tomorrow, there’ll be blood scabbed under my hair.
All senses plugged, I stare at this glass of liquor
Timing the distance until I’ll feed my head.

Waltzing down a broken road
Dragging my hands with my feet
Holding my head up by my eyes. 

Oh, how unknowing you are
Happening upon a house of holes
You fill a fifth and cover another half. 

Drowning down a sandless road
Waiting here, wishing for snow
I stare across an uncarved wall. 

Oh, how ruthless I am
I break, and when I could walk, I stand
Holding out for a warm red hand. 

Lulling along a mountainside
Upon tired leaves, spots are settling
Looking up, I see a fallen valley. 

Oh, how kind you can be
Against the foresight I’ve always had
And how contemptful I often am. 

Whistling along in the rain
Wielding my umbrella by its wings
Waiting ‘till I’m allowed to leave. 

Oh, how unhappy I’ve been
Planning how you might come around
But I’ve decided it’s time to go.

Holding this polished idol in my hand
How easy it is to let it fall
And how fleeting it is to dive back down 
It is about time, I let you go
And I know I will be back again
But I do not need you, anymore. 

Staring off into space on a Tuesday
Smiling at how your eyes were closed
And how, as you moved my hands, you smiled
Your head nodded, and I pulled
Just as your lips met mine. 

Writing tonight along candlelight
Shirt gone, feet beside a paint can
About how, just there, I had sat upside down, 
And I had held your cheeks, your hair
Feet kicked up, my eyes softly closed. 

This horrid thing I’ve held
I do not want it - and I know I’ll stumble
But there’s nothing left to do but stand;
I’ve learned, everyday, of that I dislike
Today, maybe, I’m realizing what I want to be. 

There is no other today but today
And tonight is closing, and there’s tomorrow;
I wish you were here, to lay beside me.
But right now is me - and now, I will try
So when I lie, I’ll slip off to sleep.

To others, I forward this luxury
Facilitated, through an officiation
Encouraged via happy counsel 
Or shed, and sent running along. 

This bloody heart beats not within
But like a lamp, upon a stormy shore
Run me through - I’ll go without
I’d rather you had a place to sit. 

Away from here, I release this respite,
Wipe my nose, and put up a smile;
Around here, you will see me less
Such is my only rest.

A routine day of absolute monotony 
Get to come home to love
Given abruptly, or after a roundabout.
Pictures taken affront pretty paper
With smiles formed, and planned poses
Alongside sureties expressed in speech. 

A scheduled day of absolute misery
Burned myself at least three times
And sleep is spent counting minutes. 
Every action, every activity, a distraction 
I worked so hard, for this
Who should I whore myself out for next?

A spontaneous day with a little humanity
Pull away the ladle, and strain some soup
Pour a gentle glass, and sit beside. 
Selling garbage to have more garbage
Satiating senses with similar sensations
Spending everyday waiting for tomorrow.

There are no niceties
No generosity, no politeness
Only paralysis 
And fear.
A rifle named Nitro slung across a shoulder’s blade brought about the hip laid down upon a mound of dirt and lifted to an unblinking eye.

Where is my mozambique? How is this headache and why am I living here? Eroding this particular little vice Seeking any other passing of time Edging closer to the lobe Thumb never pushing, palm never pressing. How many minutes can I count and how long am I willing to wait? When will I find my mozambique? What is my venture? But there is no question. The determinant is clear. I’ll fire the resultant over this way And then, over that way Until it settles on goodness gildless. And given it does not? Well, then fine. I will volley and relay I will send and serve and blast And if these chains remain Within, I will myself free. Reprise: Where is my life? What purpose do I serve and why am I, when it’s done? Doing the same thing as always But over here, instead of over there I am the rot never ending How narcissistic, to believe even that. All this is signatures, certificates, and emblazements; Paper burnt flutters as ash Well-inked paper, just the same.

“I don’t think I can do this anymore,”
Over a sheet of breakfast pizza
Half-poured mimosa on the table
Kids playing games on the couch
While he swears, fifteen years ago
He wanted blood relations severed
While she backs away, covers her face
And in the bathroom, allows her eyes to cry. 

It’s Christmas Time
Vacation from work, school, and the world
Wake up, and connect the cerebellum 
We rot, and then we snap it off;
We inject, and then we pretend to sleep. 

“People ask, why did he move so far away,”
Overtop the charcuterie and the spread
While they listen to him tell a story
And she sits cross-legged, face open. 
In this, there was no other option
And indeed, there are precious things I miss
But amongst friends, I imagine a muzzle flash
Remembering cold nights spent running alone. 

It’s Christmas Time
Give me coal to crack, a body to drag
I want to wake up with a reason to ache;
Picturing the fence you reached for me through
That picture of you, I’ve long since hid. 

It’s Christmas Time
And this night, spent with friends most dear
Capped off with a hammer against my head;
This sickness has spread, these fifteen years
What should I do to stop it?

You were asleep, arm laying over
You sit beside me, face looking over
You show me a picture of your girlfriend’s cat
Which was really a picture of just your cat.
I had wanted to spend time with you
I played over the internet with you
I sent pictures of my progress to you
And I am always very happy to see you. 

I don’t know what happened
Day one, I was there - now, since removed
But for all that distance, everyone else and me
There was no such distance in my heart
And I was removed nonetheless;
I sit here, waiting like a student
Laughing along in comfort only
Wishing we were all running again. 

You are the funniest person I’ve ever met
I tell people thus - and this, I still maintain. 
Some complained, and against them I stood
Praising your name, sharing our stories. 
I remember laughing until the birds sang
And laughing harder with the creeping light. 
I don’t know what happened 
How so little distance can be stretched so far.

Dreamt my mentor was wrapped around me
His hands, in my hands, on my chest
His cheek sleepy ‘tween my ear and shoulder:
I turn a little, and peak to look
His eyes are closed, and he is smiling. 
I dreamt then, as he left, I stayed
Sitting affront a screen inside an office
Classes continued, and this I knew
Every moment, I knew they were passing
And as more returned, I did not rise. 

I dreamt then of Jane Melanio
Twice, I peeked into her shadowed bedroom
She was on the right, sitting on the bed
Her boyfriend, he was laying on the left
And her best friend, Denali, on a chair beside. 
I spoke to them, and all three spoke to me
And each time I left, I wanted to talk more
But Jane and Denali both, their expressions,
they told me it was time to leave;
I will live forever with her memory. 

Television discussion on American culture
Each person, picking-up bullet shells
A base assumption, labelled revolutionary;
Last decade’s American culture is dead,
This year’s points pulled from that posed.
A lifetime ago, my wayfaring friend showed me a church
I remember, standing within that still beauty
But a year’s passed, and I finally found a smile
And before that, remember every border shattered;
If I ever pray, it will be for Jane Melanio.

I am waiting for the end of time
praying, for something to come along
thinking, maybe I ought to rhyme
together these things conjured all wrong. 

I am longing for the end of time
it is then, I am told, I won’t be sad
it is when, I know, with a sack of wine
we’ll sit together in the sand in Baghdad. 

I am imagining that story-time
dust on our skin, ash in the sky
with songs to sing, and blood to cry;
we play and hide in yesterday’s tombs
and at night, we walk and let our wounds.
During the day, we exhaust stories to tell
laying lazy on the Earth, our hearts start to swell. 

I will find the end of time
rifle practice bound, in a coffee café
amidst explosions, underneath the street

Holding me he was I’d dreamt, yesterday
I think to text - tomorrow instead, a little hello I will say. 

My head hurts so bad, today
because of what I’d done.
My head hurts so bad, today
I need to find a better way.

Marigolds have since turned yellow
in their field, the few picked left behind 
fortnights of poison, dripping from my marrow
scorched passions, settling in my mind.

Injected organisms erode my organs
darkened and dry, but taught and brought
to the start solely; laying there, dragons 
will be dead, their pretty scales left unsought. 

Thunderstorm ripping, horizon bound
man-made annihilation and generational poison.
Sorcerer rising, warlock biding, turned ‘round
she snaps and casts, stopping the solar eruption. 

Stomach and intestines sagging with holes
skin singed, feelings scarred from hellfire
sever the tendrils and the root, and fly in free fall;
light the broken anew - and I fly in free fall.

Glad you’ve the gall to express
your privilege to feel so distressed
upset, as you only now realize
my actions, never hidden from your eyes. 
I comfort you and listen to you
work you into my life, although I’ve left
and in my head, I beat myself blue
convinced all my feelings are theft. 

What tea shall you pour for me
with your collar pressed, your perfume sweet?
What message have you drafted for me? -
its delivery I await, meditating and angry.

White dress pants
a clean, white coat
with something underneath that’s nice… 
I cannot be a vampire
because I am mortal
and I have a heart. 

I miss you.
It hurts.
Nothing else matters
at all.

Here again am I 
all’s stimuli is fried
salt burning in my throat
turned - I recall how I loathe
this is truly my life

Pictures adorned over a bed frame
shadowed within a cool room quiet
A child, asking to sleep beside his mother
sleeps now only with blankets like hers. 

A picture sent of him with his parents
on either side, a smile on his face
Older, he is running around a track
in a picture, swiftly swept away. 

Pictures placed over his refrigerator 
without looking, he sees each one
The boy loves them all, in that time fleeting
time strangles on, never stopping.

A grown boy staring up
as the soft soundtrack sings through the ceiling. 
In the morning, he steps a stirrup
and sleeps the sun beneath a tree hanging. 
Southward, he works down the desert
sand squished between covered toes
one night, he hobbles out an adobe door
horse gone, he hobbles on alone. 

A grown boy leans upon cold glass
which gazes at stars beyond a distant Earth.

From broken bone mantle spills yellow
yolk unyielding, sprinkling and then streaming 
free down in heavy splashes from the ceiling,
where blotted and pressed are masses of red beaten 
muscle, manipulated and roasted nearly black. 
War lies waiting, submerging in the spreading pool 
of blood, aimed now at the rotted, bruised
cobble-sealed wall through which light’s
long since ceased to reach. 
War once hiding, encased in a cool aluminum 
shell wroughts its own writhing flame,
coerced and forced from its own crying sparks, 
aiming wrathfully the oath of atomic Hearths
ripped lengthward and snapped with thunderbolts.

Cousins cruising through glass tubes
that wind and wrap around each other
through the sky; funny quips and commentary
heard of but never seen. 

Lovers laying along the afternoon
that pass and rolls sweetly like the breeze
lying along wind chimes; sleepy days
dreamt of but never slept. 

A pretty boy with light orange hair
a kimono worn loose, tied at the waist; 
I’d like to dye my hair and sit with my leg crossed
up in a little loft, tea warm upon my lips. 
A man standing in his tower burning
his kimono pressed and tattered, he turns; 
I’d like to reign over my domain with my body bruised
from the bricks with I’d broken my bones. 

Artists arrested beneath the sewers
that snake and coil, and into each foundation
they burrow; bloody passion and obsession
fantasized but never felt.

When I die do not me attribute anything
you are not worthy of the association.

Warm radiance from your face
looking at me, your soft smile. 
Grand gradiance from this grace
hanging high above a landlocked isle. 

There is no light here, anymore.
Perhaps there was, and maybe there will be,
but light grows, reaching from a core
that itself no one can see. 

There is no light here anymore.

How far you’ve come
your feet, red and swollen.
The pain inches up, through the morning
through roots twisted and mud gone numb. 

A carefully crafted keratin shell
encases the pumping - carefully crafted
to encase limbs and cover eyes, 
and so in not seeing, come up with lies
of beauty once seen, of wonder once wanted;
and yet, outward, two antennae still fell. 

How far I’ve come
with feet red and swollen. 
Rage flaring up, into the evening
through a body and mind fallen. 

Carefully counting every combination
of a collection of characters, to be 
written in sequence - how many lives
can be sampled? How many goodbyes
to this sand for red? There’s after all a sea
well marked, but it stands in attention. 

How far I’ve gone
from food, from bedside peace
spears stuck through my skin
so my hands may hold more. 

How far I’ve gone 
from trees, from meadow’s grass
to a conglomerate collided, I study
with the kaleidoscope I arrived with.

At the warpath’s end burns a funeral pyre
built tall and slender, abandoned in Appalachia 
and its very heart mummified is a single soul 
disintegrated long before its skin had split.

Bricks cut with lasers, and stone adorned with gold
bounces back all light, but barely itself glows 
hidden away; yet in another time, there’s a hovel
with a bed made and eggs broken gentle. 

All across the land, lays the empire he’d made
negotiated and redeemed, he waited always 
to act - waiting for when it was best 
concealing his labors, and bringing his work. 
All across the land, that empire he’d made
always hidden away, although over all it reached,
and now alone, his corpse lays
as his funeral pyre slowly fades.

A pretty little ending to my everything
I’d sworn to, that which without
I would be nothing. My friends I fear
presenting myself to, bringing together
ever weary of clashes and conflicts. 
Forever recognizing the reasoning of anxiety,
of fear, heavy and constant. 
My best friend I will never abandon,
you’ve your own life filling up and lighting 
pretty, a great beginning and grace 
from the last age; I pray to God
I do not bother you, and you do not pity me.

Your bare body turned
through an invisible mirror 
softly shaken free. 
“Do you need any more help?” I asked,
and staring smiling you shook your head. 

I held your little head close 
when you called, when you wanted.
In the theater, I waited alone 
and smiling, laughing, you passed me by;
at the stairs, in the lights, you passed me by. 

So exhausted my eyelids twitch 
as warmth fills my forehead. 
You've your career, your cuffs, and no debt 
taking a psychedelic kayak down some river
sharing with people all the little thoughts you have. 

I wear a Falconer's glove 
and a thistle hat, counting coin. 
The devil my creditor, I sign
pillows and piles of rolled-up papers. 
I want to sleep, but I've not earned that. 

 A complete conviction decided
upon a light within the cave - a notion
someone else for would've been worshipped. 
Slide and show and maneuver it all
away, so we can rip it all out again. 

That era my obsession dominated 
fueled too by the convenience you fed it,
and now you'll grow fat, sitting high upon apples. 
Lots of hardship stories lead to adventures; 
I cannot leave, and I cannot die.

At the bottom of every stair fallen
and inside the center of every heart broken,
Love still remains. 

In the blood of every brutal lesson learned
and amongst the ash of every book burned,
Love still remains.

Upon the dusk of every hard year
a new light still manages to rear;
inside the storm of every fear
life crackling still manages to cheer. 
A family home, empty and abandoned
remembered by the budding couple begotten;
beaten, a worked love left dead
relaxes the heart for new love to spread. 

In the ever-darkened pit of depression
and despite every anxious decision,
Love still remains.

Split Personality
Gung-ho and boisterous

Split Personality

Split Personality

world and space, the destination

Split Personality

Run away to space
pretend to be a ranger
Hide away up North
pretend to have heritage
Hide away in a book
stare, and pretend to read
Run away to write
stare, and pretend to see. 

All I’ve written 
nobody will read
All I made up
is made up.
All I’ve written
nobody will read
All my love
no one shall receive. 

Every day my heart does not die
is another day in procrastination 
is another day alive, another day trying
one more, living for another. 
Every day my heart does not die
is twenty-four more hours survived
for seconds unseen, precious
that must come to pass.

Upon his return, he passed the test
and after his run, he purchased a gun,
a colt-barreled cobra, like the show
where they all survived shivering together. 

Pancho hung, remembering pancho lost
his head against the Dallas dust storm.
Spitting sand from his lips, he lumbers;
since set, but still not yet risen
he stands, and walks on without the sun. 

Papers published and speeches preached,
he lifts his head gentle. In the theater, 
they show that long ago he wrote. 
Alone, he whispers the soundtrack he’d played
as the python curled points up through his mouth.

Welcomed with a warm embrace, 
return marked with a drawn hello. 
Like every time, this time is closing
days counted common now dwindling. 
I will leave again, as you one day will
may we laugh together a little longer.

From behind, a gentle hold
I turn and see it’s you, 
glasses steamed, you smile
and splash me, 
and I chase you around the pool.

From our lake, a little tent 
I slowly confess to you,
declining, you smiled
and inquired,
and I managed to tease you too. 

We’re running again
for just a little bit
and sky’s shining upon you now 
hopefully for a long while. 
Once, I’d traced your peaceful brow
once, I’d prayed for the past to be now
but today, I hope the sunlight stays. 

From our hearts, a little loft
and I whisper you to sleep,
your laugh, I remember; 
Now, you’re running again, 
may you run hard.

Would I feel any different? 
Can I feel any different? 
I know what I have to do
But what the fuck do I care? 

Peace
What peace?
Everything I can pretend to be, I owe
I don’t have the luxury of being a gypsy.

In that moment, a passing glance
for reverence of beautiful grace
now gazed upon and forever, 
a blip in the report, a commission 
to lands unrazen - a machete stiff and sharp,
strapped to the hip thundering down the hill. 

The ends of these arms and feet are frozen, 
black poison ingraining across neurons
as a pink, slender snake dances, mouth open, 
fang long-embedded, the venom
kept at bay by the cold - jokes cast,
and with an axe, the snake is ripped in half. 

A half-melancholic rhythm hums
weaved within perfect the cicadas in the sun
calling down; the sad desperado waits
riding to help, to finally understand they’re too late,
the train they’d missed, now before them screams
on through the untouched temple of their dreams.

The rot that has become me
The splendor here that used to be
Shone and glimmered everywhere
And emerged waiting in every little thing
Blood broken, but not too much to bear
Life now not living, heart too heavy to bring. 

Five years burned in allegiance 
For a state that robbed me of any chance
To come or to go; you for me had waited
And I rose, and when we woke, I looked away
Destroying my life, five years I wasted
Praying with poverty a grace would fall someday. 

In this land I hate to habitate
I lay like I had with my heart and wait
A burning man in the closet, a crackled cry
For some company, a wayward’s community
But the burning man is hated forever
And when I remember him, 
I stare until he goes away. 

Breaking free from five years of hell
Disgusted, rising from this pit I dwell
You don’t want to be here, and I don’t either
I wrote all this, and maybe I’ll recall that too
to you; you had kissed my lips, and I do not care
about me
But we’re leaving nonetheless
our days left are very few
Let’s live them together
or maybe I could leave with you.

A passing sense returns to me
reminds me of youth
in its existence - there and apparent
without shield or inflation. 
And thus concludes this year’s survey,
more than a year’s past on this commission
broken by sweet three-hour tours
with people who still check-up on me. 
I believe I am well-beyond Hell
no flame could singe me worse
no pit could keep me hidden
beyond my own lashing, 
beyond my own sodomy. 
I will not yield, but there is no foe
except this shaking hand that holds the sword
cracked in the bone, aimed by the brain
and utterly detached from the heart. 
All for the sake of the three-hour future
the less I see, the more I can do
the less I think, the better I feel
the less I feel, the better I am. 

A passing sense returns to me, 
a smell amidst this absent wind
a confided whisper, later ridiculed
this sense, passes me by; by accident
upon it I stumble. My path is not its,
nor its destination mine. Yet 
in our alignment
I am alive.

My teacher, half and a decade ago
Inspired in style a soul
Unafraid to let its spirit soar
Inspired a precision aimed
not from nowhere, and toward a door
Perceived outside and felt inside. 

My teacher is being marched to the guillotine
An execution broadcasted nationally
And he faces his death standing tall
Despite their temptations to his knees;
despite their fear, he refuses to fall
They all watch, while he stands and sees.

If they can’t kill him, they’ll starve him instead
Stuffing the stomachs of the nematodic mob
Of this year’s suiting - of all I’ve been
Nothing has come close to what he taught me:
teachers gone, the world worse will be
When nobody can remember, and no one can dream.

Snow swallowing my shins
I played matador with a great Yukon yak
Out-spanned, I am smaller than his head
His shoulders behemoth, his back stone
A predictable flourish, he slammed me to the side
His tusks of horns gored me
Heavy thuds against my stomach. 

Standing again, the yak far away
I peered into a woods,
commenting it seemed rather dense
I saw then naked people, desolations strewn
between the trees; I ran for the yak
And although he had closed, they were too quick
They stretched me dismembered across the branches. 

I woke up in Newark and made for my family
In their basement, my friends had a child
A little girl who hid about the bookshelves;
In the dark lounged in a chair was my first mate
I fell and hugged her while I cried
She asked me, for three years, where I had been 
Something they did, I apologized, brought me back alive.

An ambition-planned day
by friends, so easily swept away
and comfortably too, without hurt
laughing along with the logical lecture
watching the clock, 
to on a hike stumble upon colorful fractures
that capture the sacred, and share in shards
glimpses of colors not yet named. 

However, this art is mere animation
a fondled-with algorithm, for impression
gratifying on the bewitched mind
pleasant with free-flying rhyme
so inherently a lie,
dead cold modeled warm and alive
wrapped and rolled in a sweet-seeming jive
so to convince, corral, and avoid awry. 

But there was honest wonder in that sight
in that blend of color, that seemed so right
simple, in how honest it shone
simple, it an essence like settled-bones
long laid and screwed,
a beauty hidden but still existing 
revealed in passes, deep in dreaming
And even now, that seems worth something.

I need help.
I climbed buildings.
I climbed mountains. 
I climbed thunder. 
Now what am I?

If there is God, then I am among him
He turns over his shoulder, as I walk by
I smile at him
And he nods upon me.

Every potential character 
rallied into one cold room
but one warm quart of amber
trickles slow along the neck of a loon. 
Mentorless, hopeless, on the Earth
arrived one day and made a mask
the master of tongues, the betrayer of mirth
dedicates life and love to dreamless tasks.

Could it be that I want to do something else
that this misery is not weakness but restlessness
to wander in the desert, to sleep in the street
to wake up and part and wash the sheets
default belief is that want is absurd
default response is disregarding all belief
maybe there is way that’s beyond escape
maybe there’s a life there for me.

Revealed after years of lies, the truth
and if not now, then later
very soon, time instantly passed
staring off across a campus
sitting on a swinging bench
a paper cup of piss on the sidewalk
floating extinguished, twin cigarettes. 

In what way may I take advantage
cash-in on the situation I’ve worked into 
capitalize on designed suffering. 
The optimality of the present
defending, justifying why I should still rot
instead of making some money
and maybe affording a bus pass to town. 

Broken loops piled under my bed
I’ve ripped open my leg, and I don’t want to leap 
through them anymore. 
Gut my stomach serrated across 
this on my hands, is my blood
spilled in a sink and flushed away. 

Shooting off into space, I can pretend
that somewhere out there I’ve origin
and there, I’ll finish my masterpiece. 
An armory jabbed through, what’s left since
nothing can enter, and nothing comes out
the nail approaches, ever-nearing my head. 

Drilling concentrics in frigid sand
crunching ankles against the ribs
so to feel something: how topological.
Stare at the question 
and this is all there is?
Yes.

I will take this, before you accept
and into the air I’ll let it
as swirling plumes of smoke
You hold vapor in your hand
you had refused, 
but again you almost fell
you need not let me 
you need not recognize me
I will take it instead.

What will be my latest fascination
a distraction from a non-existence. 
Existing to please, planning to be
whatever my muse wishes me to. 
A silicon stopper plugs my heart
not that there’s anything in there anyway. 
There is nothing here, and there never was
a pathetic pleating for life to be real. 
Crying to pretend that I have a feeling
a little dribbled inkling, more coping. 
A dramatic end, a flourish, for nothing
posing to make like I am something. 

An abstract for a presentation I made up
dialogue written, for pieces of my person
A love letter for someone who hurt me
life’s light, spent running in the dark
On holiday, each moment watchfully timed
a sailless ship, cast searching for the gale.

An anthmatic cinematic, well-animated and shell-shaded,
I drew and directed, all in my head
and dreaming, I replayed it twice to witness the colors clash
between two titans chasing a now-realized adversary. 

This conjured-up escape plan, imagined on inspiration
with the wind it pulled, I need though a torrent
of my own design, brutish, and maybe even falsealigned
but I need something to lean into nonetheless. 

Alone and abandoning, this mechanic iconic draconery
yet it follows me, so maybe it will never stop.
In shadows I’d wander, and turning I stared at the dark;
it’s since entered me, and I believe it’s never going to leave. 

Maybe this is my escape into a pretty little life
one where I wake, and need not make up a motive
a red mask dark, tempered and carved, will cover my face
until I am dead, and across my corpse it will remain.

How far away will you slip
going where I can’t, being where I won’t
How far can I go, and how hard may I drive
you will be, though I’ll never see you alive
Monster mash in a movie theater
glancing to my left, at my friend
Forgotten save but a name, a theatre troupe
we watched, and never again did we look.
Reading about names drawn in swirls
along the softness of sweetness’ back,
Vivacity’s invitation to unbroken rock
where power and dreams coursing arc
Not too long now, I’ll live in the valley of snow
sipping, and building with bundles of sticks
I will look to my right, and you will not be there
may your heart feel me, as mine remembers yours.

Sleep as contentment eludes me
but contentment not in relinquishment 
instead in steady focus;
may I dream of your warmth within me
my passion, my lips, upon your skin
and when I wake, may I work to make
drive ceaselessly into the setting sun
editing and tweaking, until I slip
back into a restful slumber
back into your sleeping arms.

Details indeed determined topological

enveloped in a nostalgic love
with people I’d known within the year.
How beautiful this life is
