kicking a ball for a happy dog
turning on a film and falling asleep. 

Solutions can be found it seems
and there are years out there for me
these days maybe I can live
leave them etchless upon the wall.
My stomach is glued shut, poison removed
so let this be a watershed, some broken chalk
the clasping of dust, to one day clap together hands
let this now begin.

A story about a woman who does not want to live.
Living for her friends, she wagers to leave
the continent, all her years she had been
looking for something to help her believe. 

Maybe she was throwing warmth away
or abandoning instead horrors, that left her cold
she always felt everything, so inside she stayed
and when she cried she steeled, figuring herself bold. 

But she concluded it was not boldness, but fear
and she would rise, even if it killed her
with courage into that dying, she’d chisel a spear
baked in that burning she still can’t deter. 

She arrives and she tries, meeting everyone
just like she had before, they come and they leave
and she receives them with smiles and care. 
Tonight, she ponders a fox, melting through a lake
but that she’s done and seen, all before. 

She takes on the curse of omniscience 
blended with her person, she reaches benevolence.
Now no one can hope to reach her, not that anyone could think to 
crying forever, the woman lives alone.

Laying on my back in a coasttown monastery
I watch the night through a great window
at one time, you laid with me in a mirror.
I stared, with another, at someone that was not her
self-abuse recycled, I swiftly stand
and I remember years ago, that little room
may that plight be behind me
I want to be more than this. 

The movement I made, the deeds upon I decided
I left and scaled a cliffside, and I swam in the sea
at that time, I invented myself for you.
Recorded is my rampage, ravaged were those fooled
by my illusion - but how do I explain
that Wrath, or the happiness overcoming me then?
How do I describe my desperation?
Against which errors do I justify my emotion? 

Beating heart’s fear flying from my form
a cash prize promised, in exchange for non-ideas
until I’ve studied more, I cannot learn. 
The likeness of Plato, these must be hooks
for me to hang the banner of my years; if not, 
then this theory must certainly be a failure
and for what, will I have spent my life
upon where, can I leave a marker for my legacy?

Projection to coordinates that define the sum
outlets for faults, pens for pathetic scapegoats
that measured and constructed, even during sleep
may fall within a lapsed, fleeting whim.
This heart of War compels me to passion,
that this chastinence executes an excuse;
I am horrified to live, to harp strings unsaid
to write of broken hearts, and sing of loss still loved.

A quiet boy dreams of being a space man
feeling foolish enough to laugh, people look at him
and they do not; he waits for them, and into darkness he quietly slips
and beats himself until the clock stops clicking,
chokes himself until his heart stops beating. 

A quiet boy dreamt of being a space man
but perhaps, he is destined to serve as a statesman 
of a state all his own; he will work, and of his flesh his people shall feed
and he will not be happy - but, if arises a reason to, 
he will lift others above the heavens.

Reminiscing on things from over a year ago
since then, no new thoughts, no new plans
Ambition stabbed and before the public bled
love violated and killed, the corpse hidden away. 
Hand on my thigh, horrified to decline,
would not want to upset, disrupt the schedule
At least then I’d be serving a purpose, 
satisfying somebody’s passion, fulfilling some flame. 

In another lifetime, I rolled with the night
by accident, I came upon a happy, pretty thing
Sipping tea and spirits, I’d charted constellations
through the dark and biking hard, for a hidden smile. 
Running along morning, condemning company I’d forever longed
of a wanderer further down, who wanted a companion
And it could be that was all it was, a lonely heart
holding out for a hand, hoping one he’d finally found. 

With my own hands, I strangled my throat
and the vessels there I could feel pop; no amount 
of work, can relax this hate, ultimate and supreme 
No amount of distraction, may suppress its judgement. 
Mornings toiled for wasted, living for people who don’t exist
uncovering a precious beauty, and throwing it away
This strength fades with the days; I ponder my own demise
unbefitting of such drama, antithetical to existence.

An archaic remnant of a broken phrase
I know I would protect you, and I would give you the world
and more; stories written of a lifetime damaged
of and in sympathy sent in years of letters
sealed with structures of correspondence
look, how in this anguish, I practice penmanship
look, how in this horror, I scribble shorthand. 
Grant money burned to bequeath my person
to introduce this box, which bears my skeleton 
I lift up the leagues, to wonder if you’d like lunch
I carry continents, and if need, I build new ones
so to help you to a place to stand
And I told her I would tell, if I were to hurt
but now I don’t know if I would, and I’d
rather run away, further 
and faster than anyone
so none need bear me anymore. 
Make me a mariner’s captain
loan me a rifle, and hire me amongst ex-marines
and entreat me upon securing the signature
of a faraway treaty; send me on espionage
in which I will satiate and satisfy their pleasures
always level, working to recover a manilla folder. 
When I leave to a garden in rock-sealed highlands
wherein a freak accident, I may see another person, 
or instead, I maintain alone the homestead,
and I gut myself with that chalk knife
say nothing of me, except that I did my work.

I do not want to be a daily theologian 
nor am I thus; but how may I convince myself?
Songs discovered decades ago
populate this bulletin and will not fade
because I forgot how to let them
and maybe the means are lost. 
Inject enough caffeine and I’ll teeter over
as my veins throb, finally feel awake.
Waiting for an invitation to destination agreed
you say so, but I’d rather not impose
but is this preference or paralysis 
fear of showing my face without my friend?



A novel written, but so desperate to regress
but maybe as the sun rises, I may wake. 
Allowing in a light coming through
let it flicker, and maybe it will come alive

Searing, falling, the motherboards from above 
too short, and contagious passed and pressed.
Sobbing, fighting, for a tear trickle down 
is this the battle cry of freedom?

Peeling open the hearth of needles woven
which hides the heart, cold and ashamed
and the air it smiling inhales, it spits
out one more tooth, it meticulously plucks. 
The religion is dead, its drawn parts scattered
a once embalmed emblem, beheaded and planted
upon the twisted pike before the gate; these
tongues that once sung, are now tied and strung. 
Reading of ancient orient and people beyond power
that since don’t exist, same as this, and like that before
a flag not a pole will fly, but a flag nonetheless
whether folded, or tumbling with the wind.

The precipice at the Arden
pre-meditated, your lonely plummet
but you did not fall, you hiked
far enough down the river
so that no one would see you. 
It feels blasphemic to write this
but I think about and feel
how sad and afraid, you may have been
or maybe you felt neither, or more
than just those things. 
I wish you could have told me
or anyone, and together we could’ve 
hiked that trail you had picked out
Now and before, we pick things out for you
things we shared, and things we could have done. 
You pushed so hard for me
I mouth-covered, you chanted my name
and in my misery and sickness, you nursed me.
You’d dreamt of driving far into the night
finally being free
Well, I could have driven you again. 

I hope you can see me
and I will look for you.

It is the morning of a Tuesday
in the latter half of late January 
I leave on a commission’s expedition, soon
as soon as this Winter weather blows through. 
And I am so ashamed of this shell I am
embarrassed, of these things up that I prop
I could read you the ancients, and unfold the world

And it would soar like a surfer in space
propelled peaceful by the pretty sun
shining like dusk over the mantle of our bed
big enough for one person in an old motel. 
Can you not remember, when we held each other
I remember, when we wished we could go
to somewhere anywhere, as long as it was not here
and I could carry you, and you walked with me. 
As I always have, and maybe as I always will
I bludgeon blood from my weakened brain
and I feel the fear, the doubt, the hesitation

you and I, over and into that horizon far. 
I spit blood into my filthy kitchen sink
goodbye, my dear friend 
I wish that I was better to you
It’d be nicer, if you were still here. 

I burnt a youth’s lifetime, about a boy upon the precipice 
writing a story, about somebody who just wanted to be
who never believed that they could;
there is no hatred greater
and when I break free, I cry. 
I wish you were still here
I wish you could have told me. 
I wish you were still here
I wish you could have told me.

Remember Patroclus 
The strongest of all the Greeks
Who, unto and beyond the grave
Only wanted to lay with his lover. 

Patroclus passed, underneath Hector’s spear
If Odysseus, I will not be
And if beside my Achilles, I cannot lay
Remembering them, may I fight a god. 

Against and within these shadows of columns
Erected everywhere except where they stood
This humanity is not yours, rather it is living
Like the Phaedrus, I will snap this in half. 

I remember how I cried at your hand
In bliss, that you looked and called my way
But now, cold the drill is against my skull
You won’t even return my unopened letter.

This commission’ll kill me, or it I’ll conquer
I am miserable anyway, so why not neither
Your laugh I reminisce, and your presence I miss
It seems there is no romance for me here. 

I dwell over what we did everyday
The moment there’s a break, into it you fill
Looking at you, I resolve not to burden you
With any of the pain you’ve left me with. 

Patroclus and Achilies wanted to be buried together
And centuries later, their spirits were finally united
The latter, I do not foresee from anyone
And I will pretend to have no concept of the former. 

May I like Socrates finally lash out
For a cause great and just, may I defend and stand
For an idea, an ideal, a love, I truly believe

I want to beat this misery from my brain

Against the street, fingers dug into concrete

I am devoted to self-mutilation

Telling them about you, I recall

Asyorov steps from the innards of the church.
A curved karambit, curled across his wrist
unfurls unwound, wrapping around the neck
of a rifleman patrolling the first steps of the stairs.
“Everyone, listen to me,” Asyorov screamed,
“there’s much to gain, from killing them, killing me
but just this way, follow me, and you will see
with my help - the power and wealth awaiting thee.” 

And the men and women turned to face 
the black-cloaked boy with bloodied knuckles
turned toward them, and fresh red flowing
from the knife pressed into stiffened flesh. 
From around their backs, Asyorov’s companions stared
their eyes rising, lighting lifting, bodies bared
for the opening, Asyorov will leave bored
in this broken, blued city they’ve been ensnared. 

“There is an ancient weapon, hidden just south
prophesied to render this destruction ordered
better, prettier, than the wasted raze foretold
which’ll reek phosphorous from the very ground.”
This Asyorov said; “let me share with you, its place
so your future and security, upon the piece you may base…”
but Asyorov’s vision blurred, far past his companions’ face
each person’s personage blended, against the blueing space.

In his arms the rifleman winced; blood was warm upon Asyorov’s wrist.
“Suppose we follow you,” someone said, “what will us await?
Is its appointment atomic? - or rather, is it electronic
or maybe more subtle - a grand manipulator, a great lawbringer?”
Asyorov’s arm flashed, and the man’s body fell
heavy against the cobblestone, as Asyorov ceased to sell;
black cloak over the curved karambit - overhead the bell
declared into the day: Asyorov is an infidel.

Sleep pierces in
drags out barbed and poisoned
ripped free from a ripped-up couch
tumbling upon vacation’s end. 

A dull, vague, blank thumping
interchanging with a bright ringing
at the internal’s sarcastic leisure
at the internal’s sadistic hunger. 

Desperatism labeled wrongly,
libelled into evil, accused of arson
convicted and executed soundly. 
Sadism’s meanwhile being pardoned
lobeyed the victim, shielded by smiles
who show at most but one dripping tear. 

Tomorrow commences my nation’s odyssey.
In a half-decade, maybe I’ll finally leave
upon my own, in a vessel I’ve commissioned
or maybe as a mysterious stowaway
sleeping beneath the stern, chewing lobster shells. 
Passionate sepharim, chained without a vassal 
blades missing and face bloody, He stares
as I glance, speeding along my hand-built rail;
where once my back scabbed has turned black
and, not at all like the flames steady beneath me,
my feathers have long since been burnt. 

There is a reality where I live my own years.
Sweaty, I shove a shovel’s blade into the Earth
drop my gloves and grab hold tight of some rope stiff
and with my own weight, the wooden boat falls and settles
into that sea’s expanse not so calm. 
And as I row steadily upon waves spreading
I look over my shoulder and see dark and brooding
the so-called brimstone on the horizon gathering;
I peak underneath, and there a great eye awaits -
an abyssal beast studying me, just as I study him.
Perhaps I join the leviathan, and the leviathan joins me
and we together fight on, for our just-joint journey,
or maybe, the leviathan yanks me under simply,
or I maneuver, just to be by the storm devoured.
At least the end will be my own.

passive ideations of suicide remain on the mind
always and patiently, waiting with a bullet aimed 
at the roof of my mouth. There is no greatness here,
no innocence, no honesty. All contrived and made up and
realigned parallel with some other neon light in the sky. 
None of what I have written here are poems
and my massive useless manuscript certainly isn’t a story.
How must it feel to act without control, to reach 
and touch and pull something wanted and not coveted 
for emptiness’ distraction - for the conviction that 
this destination is the final salvation sensation? How must it feel
to not wake up with ears already ringing, the mind already begging
for dull, vibrating stimming to obscure the pained droning
of a skull-case against a wrinkled mash of nothing,
which calls for a quick and violent arresting as there 
is no real want, no real sanctity, no real goodness. 
I have been motivated and have written a closing stanza-block
for these sorts of things, configuring optimism and resilience into this 
whining; but the truth instead may be that I do not want to feel this badly
anymore, and so even if this one day becomes optimism, right now and 
for now it is desperation from the soul of a heartbroken addict, 
and optimism and hope resemble not at all any beating I can 
gaslight myself into rendering.

There are things that I like despite all the aspects of my incessant self-destruction.
Not any must-be’s or ought-to-be’s, just simply there are’s;
there exists that which could constitute a real life spent.

May our shadows fall quiet now

forced to be stiff, forced to shine
akin to that spectrum bound limited
alike to that dullity they sleep with. 

The imposter hidden
within her own skin, invisible
within herself, unknown and torn;

stabbed through with road signs
slapped over with orange paint,

and the massive, holdless and indivisible.

but I cannot is argued by some stone stabbed into my head
and all that wetness, all the tenderness, cannot burn
it is compacted, violated, and against a block impaled
for when I’m feeling like stamping some more self-harm. 

With an ounce of self-respect reminded is the condone 
my existence is an insult to the solar-structure
through which you filter and methadone your palindrome 
that you all share and gestate and yourselves you cover
lifting it up in dry hands, expecting in return a bowl. 

A bowl of liquid and plasma gold, shining like mercury
in which you will bathe, and with which will lather
and in each other you will stare and gaze and wonder
how beautiful this life can be, how ornate this essence has to be
so that it may be so humbly witnessed by scientists such as we. 

I want to be better, to help, to maybe be a hero
but that is a distraction too, from the never-ending beared,
holding my breath and tightening the wrapped red linen
I lie, because I have no answer

I love you, she whispered
her face facing mine, but covered
with her hands. I love you too, 
I say. But you’d said you wanted 
to be with a man, she said, 
how can this be so? 
And even amongst my dream,
I could say nothing.
This thing you are running from, 
she said, you cannot run far enough;
she hit her own head against the wall, 
it hurts so much because I know you are good,
she said, as I cried, shaking on her bed
after she pulled it out of me. 
But never did I let know, my obsession
given opportunity suddenly sensible. 
A tower I’d imagined to be her throne,
under the red dark-light, she leaned
whispering of my lips, and I held her shoulder,
but come sobriety’s return, so did calculation
and so, eyes wide open, I let my body burn. 
That I took from grace, may I sell of myself
this scheming operatic, may I finally rip free. 
Daring the blade above my neck, I temper
this oppressed anger, this contempful fear
so I may leave. 
You are scary smart, she said,
reading what I’d wrote, and worlds apart
we could have shared that and more. 

The man down the hall, she said, is talking to me
a kind of, rebound thing, which was fun 
but now, I don’t want him around. 
I remember, now, how’d she’d told me, 
I was wishing I was hanging with you. 
Love bewitched and swindled, demanded
and expected; this body, and this time,
given absolutely and without conscience
and all this, without the recognition
of the cunning construction of a running machine
the contraption of entrapment, 
a straw man, for amor and ambition. 
But love happened upon on a nightlit walk
leaning drunk together at the base of a post
a lifetime of dormant truth, rising to the sun
and waking with it wrapped together. 
And against its glory, I stepped back once
in that gap, my partner fled;
compiled were, a year’s of letters, plans, and dreams
behind my door drawn closed. 
I prefer experienced men, he laughed before me
to friends I figured we’d share
I drove into the desert, passing over the sand
and since, every quiet, fleeting thought
every effort made, reaching for ways to move on
was spent on someone who only ever had places to go. 

This thing I am running from, she said to me
I can never really get away - in-stride 
I exacted vengeance; or perhaps, a lapse in passion
with someone who felt it too
was a desperate attempt to step away from sadness. 
He was interested in staying with me
and I declined honestly, so perhaps I am closer 
to that thing I am running to.
